ChatGPT has now a serious competitor.

Anthropic has just released Claude 2 and it's really powerful.

Here's why you should use Claude 2 instead of ChatGPT (for free):

[Thread]

Anthropic isn’t just building chatbots
They’re building a system that can think, code & execute

And most people are using it wrong.

There are actually 3 different Claudes — and each one replaces a different type of work:

1. Claude AI → replaces Google + docs + junior

U.S. Sen. Elizabeth Warren is questioning the Defense Department about its supply chain risk designation on Anthropic as Claude continues to be used in the Iran war. Warren said the move by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth “appears to be retaliation."

The Guardian: Anthropic vs Pentagon: AI drama in a California courtroom. Claude plays defense, while drones and budgets hover in the wings. Europe peers at the clash—can Nordic AI ethics survive a US tech tiff over autonomy and surveillance? Stay tuned.
